response.justification: Strong geospatial domain expertise and proven ability to build scalable, distributed systems make this candidate valuable for routing pipeline work. However, the significant gap in modern C++ experience (only ~6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015) and complete lack of Rust knowledge present a material risk, given C++ is the primary required skill. The candidate's background in D and Node.js systems suggests they could learn C++, but would require substantial ramp-up time. This is borderline: suitable for teams prioritizing domain knowledge and general engineering quality, but not ideal if immediate C++ productivity is critical.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and Green Map navigation projects addressing real-world use cases
Proven track record building high-performance, distributed systems at scale (Dunnhumby microservices handling millions of requests/second)
Strong software quality culture—proficiency in TDD, test frameworks, CI/CD, and teaching software craftsmanship practices
concerns: Minimal modern C++ experience (only ~6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015), which is the primary required skill
No Rust experience despite role requiring C++ or Rust as a core competency
Career trajectory heavily weighted toward Node.js, TypeScript, and DLang—systematically moving away from systems programming languages

key_factors.factor: Distributed systems and performance optimization experience
